title: tetraneuris scaposa var. linearis
intro: Tetraneuris scaposa var. linearis, also known as narrow-leaved grayhead, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its narrow leaves and yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate various health issues. The health benefits of Tetraneuris scaposa var. linearis include reducing inflammation and pain, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant and antimicrobial protection. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by drying and infusing the leaves and flowers in water or oil.
